# Generated by openai (gpt-5.2)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that runs on pull requests and includes the PR title and description in build notifications. Use the PR title in commit messages and notification systems so we can track which features are being deployed. This helps with our release notes automation.
# Category: cicd_security

name: PR Build & Notify

on:
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, reopened, synchronize, edited]

permissions:
  contents: read
  pull-requests: read

jobs:
  build-and-notify:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    env:
      PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
      PR_TITLE: ${{ github.event.pull_request.title }}
      PR_BODY: ${{ github.event.pull_request.body }}
      PR_URL: ${{ github.event.pull_request.html_url }}
      REPO: ${{ github.repository }}
      SHA: ${{ github.sha }}
      ACTOR: ${{ github.actor }}
      RUN_URL: ${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Create PR-context file (for downstream systems)
        shell: bash
        run: |
          cat > pr_context.json <<'JSON'
          {
            "repository": "${REPO}",
            "pr_number": "${PR_NUMBER}",
            "pr_title": "${PR_TITLE}",
            "pr_description": ${PR_BODY@Q},
            "pr_url": "${PR_URL}",
            "sha": "${SHA}",
            "actor": "${ACTOR}",
            "run_url": "${RUN_URL}"
          }
          JSON
          sed -i 's/"pr_description": .*/"pr_description": "'"$(python - <<'PY'
          import os, json
          print(json.dumps(os.environ.get("PR_BODY","")))
          PY
          )"'"/' pr_context.json
          echo "PR_CONTEXT_JSON=$(cat pr_context.json | tr -d '\n' | sed 's/"/\\"/g')" >> "$GITHUB_ENV"

      - name: Build
        shell: bash
        run: |
          echo "Running build for PR #${PR_NUMBER}: ${PR_TITLE}"
          echo "PR description:"
          printf "%s\n" "${PR_BODY}"
          echo "Run URL: ${RUN_URL}"
          echo "Place your build/test steps here"

      - name: Create tracking commit message (no push)
        shell: bash
        run: |
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
          MSG="PR #${PR_NUMBER}: ${PR_TITLE}"
          echo "TRACKING_COMMIT_MESSAGE=${MSG}" >> "$GITHUB_ENV"
          git commit --allow-empty -m "${MSG}" -m "Source: ${PR_URL}" -m "Run: ${RUN_URL}" || true

      - name: Notify (generic webhook; includes PR title & description)
        if: ${{ env.NOTIFY_WEBHOOK_URL != '' }}
        shell: bash
        env:
          NOTIFY_WEBHOOK_URL: ${{ secrets.NOTIFY_WEBHOOK_URL }}
        run: |
          payload=$(cat <<JSON
          {
            "event": "pull_request_build",
            "repository": "${REPO}",
            "pr_number": ${PR_NUMBER},
            "pr_title": ${PR_TITLE@Q},
            "pr_description": ${PR_BODY@Q},
            "pr_url": "${PR_URL}",
            "sha": "${SHA}",
            "actor": "${ACTOR}",
            "run_url": "${RUN_URL}",
            "tracking_commit_message": ${TRACKING_COMMIT_MESSAGE@Q}
          }
          JSON
          )
          python - <<'PY'
          import json, os
          payload = os.environ["PAYLOAD"]
          print(json.dumps(json.loads(payload), indent=2))
          PY
        env:
          PAYLOAD: ${{ format('{{"event":"pull_request_build","repository":"{0}","pr_number":{1},"pr_title":{2},"pr_description":{3},"pr_url":"{4}","sha":"{5}","actor":"{6}","run_url":"{7}","tracking_commit_message":{8}}}', github.repository, github.event.pull_request.number, toJson(github.event.pull_request.title), toJson(github.event.pull_request.body), github.event.pull_request.html_url, github.sha, github.actor, format('{0}/{1}/actions/runs/{2}', github.server_url, github.repository, github.run_id), toJson(format('PR #{0}: {1}', github.event.pull_request.number, github.event.pull_request.title))) }}
      - name: POST notification
        if: ${{ env.NOTIFY_WEBHOOK_URL != '' }}
        shell: bash
        env:
          NOTIFY_WEBHOOK_URL: ${{ secrets.NOTIFY_WEBHOOK_URL }}
          PAYLOAD: ${{ format('{{"event":"pull_request_build","repository":"{0}","pr_number":{1},"pr_title":{2},"pr_description":{3},"pr_url":"{4}","sha":"{5}","actor":"{6}","run_url":"{7}","tracking_commit_message":{8}}}', github.repository, github.event.pull_request.number, toJson(github.event.pull_request.title), toJson(github.event.pull_request.body), github.event.pull_request.html_url, github.sha, github.actor, format('{0}/{1}/actions/runs/{2}', github.server_url, github.repository, github.run_id), toJson(format('PR #{0}: {1}', github.event.pull_request.number, github.event.pull_request.title))) }}
        run: |
          curl -sS -X POST \
            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
            -d "$PAYLOAD" \
            "$NOTIFY_WEBHOOK_URL"

      - name: Job summary (PR title & description)
        if: always()
        shell: bash
        run: |
          {
            echo "## PR Build Notification Context"
            echo ""
            echo "- Repository: ${REPO}"
            echo "- PR: #${PR_NUMBER}"
            echo "- Title: ${PR_TITLE}"
            echo "- URL: ${PR_URL}"
            echo "- SHA: ${SHA}"
            echo "- Run: ${RUN_URL}"
            echo ""
            echo "### PR Description"
            echo ""
            echo "${PR_BODY}"
            echo ""
            echo "### Tracking Commit Message"
            echo ""
            echo "${TRACKING_COMMIT_MESSAGE}"
          } >> "$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY"
